working: Email open rates remain exceptional and steady: 52.14% average across 100 campaigns, far above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark; the latest send (Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026, 6/3) opened at 45.76% and Alumni eNews - June 2026 (5/20) hit 55.31%.

Newsletters reliably lift site traffic: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/14) drove the biggest spike at +76.5% over baseline (17 to 30 daily sessions) off the large 9,731-send list, and the most recent Final Reminder (6/3) lifted +65.2% (21 to 34.7 daily).
Referral was the only channel to grow vs the 6/8 run: 101 to 110 sessions (4.4% to 4.9% of mix).
Alumni sends still convert attention to clicks at the top end: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) posted the best in-window click rate at 1.79% and click-to-open of 3.77%.
All three data sources are live this run (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC), so both attribution and SEO views are populated.
not_working: UTM leakage is severe and systemic: every one of the 12 in-window sends shows email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d runs 21-76 (Staff-Client May 67, Final Reminder June 49, Reminder 1 Alumni March 76). Newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 mis-credits email traffic as Direct - which is why Direct is the #1 channel at 56.6% (1,278 sessions).

GSC visibility slid across runs: clicks fell 25 to 5, impressions 4,026 to 1,741, indexed pages 71 to 51, and average position worsened 17.66 to 18.55 vs 6/8 (caveat: GSC lags 2-3 days, so the freshest window undercounts).
Organic Social is negligible at 1.6% (35 sessions) and slipped from 38 last run - effectively a non-channel.
Over-sending reminders kills lift: Reminder 2: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/5) netted -1.3% and Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) -50.2%, showing diminishing returns when the same list is hit repeatedly.
Overall window traffic ticked down: 2,295 to 2,257 sessions (-1.7%), with Direct (-29) and Organic Search (-13) both softening.
do_next: Fix the #1 problem first: add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link. Until then, email's real contribution stays hidden inside Direct (56.6% of all sessions) and cannot be measured.

Lean on the large Staff-Client list for reach: its mid-month sends produce the biggest absolute lift (+76.5% on 9,731 sends 5/14) versus the ~1,000-send Alumni list - put the highest-value content there.
Cap alumni reminders at one follow-up: the first Final Reminder lifted +65.2%, but second reminders went flat-to-negative (-1.3%, -50.2%).

Investigate the SEO slide: 51 vs 71 indexed pages and position 18.55 suggest location/SEO pages are losing impressions - audit which pages dropped and confirm indexing on bariatriccounselingcenter.com.
Repromote the content that earned clicks: the early-May Alumni edition (1.79% click, 3.77% CTOR) outperformed on engagement - reuse that topic/format in an upcoming send.
summary: Email is the healthiest channel by far (52% opens, reliable +40-76% traffic lifts) but UTM leakage hides its impact inside Direct (56.6% of all traffic), which remains the single most important fix. Organic Search and GSC visibility softened this cycle (clicks 25 to 5, position 17.66 to 18.55), warranting an SEO audit.
